title: SAMH Youthreach
text:
SAMH Youthreach - SupportGoWhere
SAMH Youthreach
Share link
Provides psychosocial support and recovery programmes for youths aged 12 to 21 with emotional, psychological and psychiatric issues in the community.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Centre-based
Free
Referral required
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

The programme staff work with youths and their families to minimise risk, promote safety and improve functioning.

Services include:

Counselling

Case management

Family psychoeducation programme

Expressive therapies

Psycho-social activities

Who is this for?

Singapore Citizens or Permanent Residents aged 21 and below with emotional and psychological challenges

No recent history of violence, suicide attempts, alcohol or substance abuse, and sexual acting out behaviours

Willing to participate in the centre’s activities

Family is willing to participate

What to expect?

An intake interview with child/youth and caregiver will be arranged to assess suitability.

Upon successful registration, the child/youth and caregiver will be engaged for services including case management, psychoeducation and psychosocial development programmes.

How to apply?

Referral by a psychiatrist, school counsellor, or social worker using the SAMH referral form is required.

Information last updated on 10 Nov 2025
